data.title: Market Chatter: Alibaba Affiliate Ant's October-December 2024 Profit Slides 31%
data.description:
Ant Group's net profit fell 31% year over year to about 5.3 billion yuan in the three months ended Dec. 31, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing calculations by the news agency based on Alibaba's earnings report.

Ant contributed 1.76 billion yuan in profit to Alibaba in the quarter, according to Bloomberg.

As Alibaba holds one-third of Ant's stake, Ant logged an estimated profit of 5.3 billion yuan, the report said.

Ant's earnings are one quarter behind Alibaba's, the report said.

data.title: SUNation Energy Announces Q1 2025 Results
data.description:
SUNation Energy, Inc., a prominent provider of sustainable solar energy solutions, has released its financial results for the first quarter of 2025. The company has made significant strides in debt reduction, cost containment, and enhancing cash flow, despite facing some challenges in revenue growth.

Financial Highlights

For the quarter ended March 31, 2025, SUNation Energy reported a consolidated revenue decline of 4% to $12.6 million from $13.2 million in the same period last year. The company's gross profit stood at $4.4 million, representing a gross margin of 35.1%, down from 36.4% in Q1 2024. Selling, general, and administrative (SG&A) expenses decreased by 9% to $6.0 million, reflecting the company's cost optimization efforts. However, the company reported a net loss of $(3.5) million compared to a net income of $1.2 million in Q1 2024.

Business and Operational Highlights

SUNation's commercial project backlog increased by over 30% as of March 31, 2025, compared to the same period last year. The New York Residential business faced seasonal headwinds due to poor weather in February but saw a strong recovery in the spring. The Hawaii Energy Connection segment experienced an 11% decline in revenue, attributed to the lack of solar and battery incentives in Q1 2025, which were reinstated on May 15, 2025.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

The company has successfully reduced its total debt by 51% to $9.2 million from $19.1 million at the end of 2024. Additionally, SUNation secured $20 million in gross proceeds through a securities purchase agreement with institutional investors. The company also eliminated $12.6 million of secured debt and other long-term obligations, reducing its annual cash drain by approximately $3.4 million through 2027.

Management's Perspective

Scott Maskin, Chief Executive Officer, highlighted the company's progress in corporate transformation activities, including cost containment, operating efficiencies, and debt reduction. He expressed optimism about the company's long-term prospects and its ability to capitalize on the growing demand for solar energy.

Future Outlook

For the full year 2025, SUNation Energy has provided financial guidance, projecting total sales of $65 million to $70 million, representing a 14% to 23% increase from 2024. The company also expects an adjusted EBITDA of $0.5 million to $0.7 million, an improvement from the adjusted EBITDA loss in 2024. The guidance is based on current business conditions and does not account for potential impacts from tariffs, global disruptions, or changes in trade policies.

SEC Filing: SUNation Energy, Inc. [ PEGY ] - 8-K - May. 15, 2025

data.title: KB Components appoints Michael Grindborn as new Chief Financial Officer
data.description:
Michael Grindborn has been appointed CFO of KB Components and will take up the position (at the latest) on November 15, 2025. Michael comes most recently from the role of CFO at Balco Group and brings a solid experience to the company, including as Group CFO at Lammhults Design Group. He has spent most of his career as CFO, with many years in HEXPOL and its business area Engineered Products.

Michael holds a Master's degree in Business Administration from Bocconi, Milan.

"We are very pleased to welcome Michael Grindborn to KB as CFO. His extensive expertise in finance and economics, combined with a proven track record of successful leadership in listed companies, adds valuable strategic and operational insights that will support the company in driving sustainable profitable growth.", says Stefan Andersson, CEO

During the period until Michael joins KB Components, the company has engaged an interim consultant with good experience of the Group.

Stefan Andersson

CEO

data.title: Jones Soda Co. Q1 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
Jones Soda Co., a leading craft soda manufacturer, has released its financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025. The company, which also has a subsidiary dedicated to cannabis products, provided insights into its financial performance, strategic initiatives, and management's perspective on the results.

Financial Highlights

For the first quarter of 2025, Jones Soda reported a revenue of $4.6 million, a slight decrease from $5.0 million in the same period last year. The net loss for the quarter decreased to $0.9 million, or $(0.01) per share, compared to a net loss of $1.2 million, or $(0.01) per share, in the first quarter of 2024. Adjusted EBITDA improved by 39% to $(0.6) million from $(1.0) million, primarily due to a reduction in selling, general, and administrative expenses.

Business and Operational Highlights

Jones Soda's beverages segment, which includes craft soda, HD9, Pop Jones, and Fiesta Jones brands, generated approximately $4.2 million in revenue. The company's Cannabis (THC) segment, under the Mary Jones brand, generated approximately $380,000 in revenue. Notably, HD9 sales reached $0.9 million, marking the fourth consecutive quarter of sales expansion.

In terms of product launches, Jones Zero Cola was introduced in March across over 10,000 national and regional grocery stores, with plans to launch additional zero-calorie flavors later in the year. Additionally, Pop Jones is now featured in Modern Beverage POGs across over 1500 national and regional chain stores.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

During the first quarter, Jones Soda announced the hiring of Scott Harvey as the new Chief Executive Officer and Brian Meadows as the new Chief Financial Officer. The company also secured a $5 million revolving credit facility to support strategic initiatives for expansion and sales growth. Furthermore, the company reduced both selling and marketing and general and administrative expenses by 20% without any apparent productivity dilution.

Management's Perspective

Scott Harvey, CEO of Jones Soda, commented, "The first quarter of 2025 was the beginning of the strategic turnaround for Jones, and we are off to a good start. Since stepping into leadership in February, we’ve moved quickly to implement meaningful improvements across key areas of the business. We optimized our supply chain operations, tightened P&L oversight, and instilled disciplined cost management measures which have already begun to yield significant results."

He added, "We remain focused on executing our strategic plan with clear, measurable goals to scale the business while preserving the beloved Jones brand and image. We have leaned into unlocking more efficiencies across our three main categories – Core Soda, Modern Soda, and Adult Beverage - through investing in high-growth opportunities and managing our cost structure with disciplined, ROI-driven decisions."

Future Outlook

Jones Soda is committed to continuing its strategic turnaround with a focus on operational rigor and cost management. The company plans to introduce additional zero-calorie flavors and explore strategic alternatives for its Cannabis THC business. With strong industry momentum and a dedicated team, the company is optimistic about its future prospects.

SEC Filing: JONES SODA CO [ JSDA ] - 8-K - May. 15, 2025

data.title: Ethylene prices journey southward in Europe
data.description: This week, ethylene prices down adjusted in the European region.An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member, "The ethylene markets in Europe faced a lacklustre trading atmosphere, marked by minimal spot transactions. Market participants adopted a cautious approach, largely remaining inactive, which resulted in few trades and low trading volumes. This subdued activity indicates a phase of market uncertainty or stability, as both buyers and sellers were reluctant to participate actively, likely due to ambiguous market fundamentals, varying feedstock prices, or broader economic influences affecting supply and demand. In summary, the market was quiet, with little price fluctuation or notable activity during this timeframe. The source added, "Market participants opted to stay on the sidelines, avoiding active trading or significant investments. This cautious approach was mainly influenced by ongoing developments concerning tariffs, which added a significant level of uncertainty to the market landscape. As investors and traders kept a close watch on these developments, their reluctance mirrored concerns regarding potential effects on prices, supply chains, and overall market stability. The ongoing uncertainty maintained elevated market volatility, resulting in a period of heightened caution and diminished market participation among participants. Market activity in North West Europe (NWE) has generally been sluggish, with a more significant drop observed in the CIF NWE market compared to the FD NWE market. Participants and sources in the market reported limited discussions about imports, indicating a cautious or hesitant approach among buyers. Furthermore, there was a lack of interest from buyers in pursuing new import transactions. Concerns regarding potential specification issues related to imported materials were also mentioned as a contributing factor to the subdued activity, which may have deterred wider engagement in imports and fostered an overall cautious market atmosphere.On Thursday, spot ethylene prices were assessed at Euro 690-700/mt FD Northwest Europe, a week on week steep drop of Euro (-45/mt). CIF NWE ethylene prices were assessed at USD 745-755/mt, a fall of USD (-20/mt) from the previous week.The supply situation was described as plentiful to some extent, with a tendency towards slight overabundance, despite producers operating at reduced cracker run rates. This indicates that although production levels remained low, the overall availability of the product surpassed demand. Producers seemed to lack motivation to increase their output, mainly due to financial constraints, as some industry sources reported that profit margins were under pressure. This combination of cautious production and limited incentive to enhance output contributed to the overall supply dynamics, resulting in a slight oversupply in the market. The market is largely stagnant as participants await the 90-day suspension of US-EU tariffs. The uncertainty regarding the demand outlook has significantly reduced activities. Participants are concentrating on essential needs only, maintaining low inventories and avoiding risks associated with additional business. There is a growing mention of derivative imports, primarily from the US, which will exert downward pressure on derivative margins and dampen domestic demand for ethylene. Concerns over potential retaliatory tariffs on US polyethylene (PE) imposed last month provided a temporary boost to domestic producers, but this advantage faded with the US and EU's decision to suspend the implementation of the tariffs.The European ethylene contract price for May 2025 settled at the Euro 1135/MT FD North West Europe levels. This price represents a plunge of Euro 70/MT from its April 2025 settlement levels.

data.title: Apax Funds Begins Compulsory Buyout for Remaining Norva24 Group Shares
data.description:
Apax Funds, via Nordahl BidCo, started compulsory redemption proceedings to acquire the remaining shares it does not own in Norva24 Group .

The bidder holds a 98.32% stake in the Swedish infrastructure maintenance company, as announced on May 7. Its 36.50 kronor per share tender offer was announced March 10.

Norva24 Group applied to delist its shares on Nasdaq Stockholm, with its final trading date to be announced once confirmed by the exchange.

data.title: Stocks, FX set for weekly gains; Russia-Ukraine talks awaited
data.description:
Key points:
  • MSCI EM stocks, FX flat on the day
  • Romania cenbank verdict, weekend elections in focus
  • Poland's presidential elections expected on May 18
  • Trump announces many deals during Gulf tour

Indexes tracking stocks and currencies of major emerging markets were steady on Friday, but set to rise for a fifth straight week, while investors monitored the Russia-Ukraine talks and political developments in central and eastern Europe.

MSCI's index tracking stocks CBOE:EFS was set for a weekly rise of 2.9%, while its currencies gauge (.MIEM00000CUS) was on track for advances of 0.3% against the dollar.

Global markets rallied earlier in the week as the U.S. struck trade deals with the UK, along with a tariff truce with China, which greatly reduced the risk of a global recession.

However, investors remain uncertain of what impact the newly-negotiated tariffs, which are still higher than in late 2024, could have on the world economy.

Investors are now turning their eyes to talks between Russian and Ukrainian negotiators in Istanbul, with both parties under pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump to end Europe's deadliest conflict since World War Two.

A meeting between Turkish, U.S. and Ukrainian officials is ongoing and talks between delegations from the three countries are expected at 0930 GMT.

Ukrainian international bonds (XS1303929894=TE), (XS2895057334=TE) inched higher after declining marginally earlier in the week, when it became clear that Russian President Vladimir Putin would skip the negotiations and sent a mid-level delegation instead.

The rouble depreciated 0.8%, tracking weaker crude prices.

In central and eastern Europe, Romania's leu was steady against the euro, while yields on the 10-year bond (RO10YT=RR) fell about 35 basis points ahead of a central bank interest rate decision later in the day.

"Political turmoil is a major constraint on easing monetary policy. A weaker currency is likely to have inflationary consequences, which would limit scope for the central bank to seriously consider a rate cut in the coming months should Simion become president," said Piotr Matys, senior FX analyst at In Touch Capital Markets.

The central bank propped up the leu a week ago, after hard-right presidential candidate George Simion's victory in a first-round vote sparked a selloff and pushed borrowing costs higher. Worries are that Simion's policies could exacerbate the country's fiscal and trade balances.

Meanwhile, Poland's zloty was also muted as all eyes shifted to the presidential elections on May 18, with opinion polls showing ruling party Civic Coalition's Rafal Trzaskowski in the lead.

Also in focus was U.S. President Donald Trump's Gulf tour where through the week, where he signed billion-dollar deals with Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates.

Trump also said the U.S. and the UAE have created a path for the Gulf country to buy some of the most advanced artificial intelligence semiconductors.

Trump also announced plans to lift sanctions on Syria and said a deal with Iran was underway.

data.title: CAMP4 Presents Translational Data from SYNGAP1-Related Disorders Program Showcasing Increased Protein in Non-Human Primates and Reviews Preclinical and Detailed Single Ascending Dose Safety Data from Urea Cycle Disorders Program at the 28th American Society of Gene and Cell Therapy Annual Meeting
data.description:
Haploinsufficient SYNGAP1 mice treated with CMP-SYNGAP-01 demonstrated an increase in SYNGAP1 protein levels; treatment rescued multiple SYNGAP1-dependent behavioral phenotypes

CMP-SYNGAP-01 administration led to a significant increase in SYNGAP1 protein levels in relevant brain regions in non-human primates (NHPs)

Patient safety and pharmacokinetic data from single ascending dose (SAD) cohorts of the first-in-human Phase 1 clinical trial of CMP-CPS-001 in healthy volunteers highlighted

CAMBRIDGE, Mass., May 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — CAMP4 Therapeutics Corporation (“CAMP4”) , a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing a pipeline of regulatory RNA-targeting therapeutics designed to upregulate gene expression with the goal of restoring healthy protein levels to treat a broad range of genetic diseases, today delivered three oral presentations on its SYNGAP1-related disorders and Urea Cycle Disorders (UCDs) programs and shared favorable safety and pharmacokinetics data from the ongoing Phase 1 trial of CMP-CPS-001 in healthy volunteers at the 28th Annual Meeting of the American Society of Gene and Cell Therapy, taking place in New Orleans, May 13 – 17, 2025.

“Patients living with SYNGAP1-related disorders and UCDs currently face a critical dearth of disease-modifying treatment options to manage their condition,” said Dan Tardiff, Ph.D., Senior Vice President, Head of Discovery at CAMP4. “These proof-of-mechanism data indicate CMP-SYNGAP-01 can restore SYNGAP1 protein levels to mitigate disease-relevant phenotypes in haploinsufficient mice and increase SYNGAP1 protein in disease-relevant brain regions in non-human primates when delivered by the clinical route of administration. Additionally, our UCD clinical candidate is well tolerated, and we are preparing to evaluate CMP-CPS-001 in OTC heterozygotes, a population with reduced urea cycle function. We’re excited to continue pioneering our novel approach of upregulating gene expression and addressing the unmet needs of many patients living with genetic diseases characterized by haploinsufficiency or recessive loss of function.”

Josh Mandel-Brehm, Chief Executive Officer of CAMP4, added, “These compelling data underscore the expansive potential of our RAP Platform to address a wide spectrum of genetic conditions, starting with neurologic and metabolic disorders. By pairing clinically validated antisense technologies with newly discovered regulatory RNA targets to upregulate gene expression, we have an opportunity to rapidly advance therapeutics for disorders characterized by insufficient protein production including SYNGAP1-related disorders, where there is significant unmet need for disease-modifying therapies. We look forward to progressing toward additional clinical trials and exploring strategic partnerships that can accelerate our development plans and deliver long-term value for patients and shareholders.”

Key findings for each program are as follows:

SYNGAP1-related disorders program

  • In haploinsufficient mice carrying a single copy of the human SYNGAP1 gene, intracerebroventricular (ICV) injection of CMP-SYNGAP-01, a development candidate targeting a regulatory RNA sequence mapped to a SYNGAP1 gene regulatory region, resulted in:
    • Restored SYNGAP1 protein levels to near normal range after a single dose
    • Rescue of motor defects and spatial learning defects following two doses
  • In NHPs, biweekly intrathecal injections of CMP-SYNGAP-01 resulted in a ~1.5-fold increase in SYNGAP1 protein levels across multiple brain regions clinically relevant to the disease
    • Dose-linear increase in CMP-SYNGAP-01 in disease-relevant brain regions
    • CMP-SYNGAP-01 was well tolerated

UCD program

  • Preclinical data
    • In Otc-deficient mice, treatment with CMP-CPS-001 resulted in dose-dependent reductions in ammonia levels, which persisted for approximately 4 weeks
      • Increases in mRNA levels of additional enzymes of the urea cycle were observed, suggesting increased metabolic activity
    • In mice with humanized livers, administration of CMP-CPS-001 following an ammonia challenge resulted in increased ureagenesis and decreased ammonia levels
    • Administration of CMP-CPS-001 in NHPs resulted in up to 40% increase in ureagenesis, supporting the MOA to convert ammonia to urea
  • Phase 1 clinical data
    • 48 healthy adult participants were enrolled across four SAD cohorts and randomized 3:1 to a single subcutaneous dose of CMP-CPS-001, with 36 participants randomized to CMP-CPS-001
    • CMP-CPS-001 was well tolerated, with no evidence of a maximum tolerated dose and no safety trends of concern
    • All treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs) were Grade 1 (mild) or Grade 2 (moderate) with no serious or severe adverse events (AEs) or TEAEs and no participants discontinued study drug due to a TEAE
      • Most common TEAEs were headache (n=6) followed by nausea (n=4)
    • Pharmacokinetics
      • Dose-dependent increase in exposure (Cmax and AUC) with clear separation between dose levels
      • Greater than dose-proportional increase in exposure (Cmax and AUC0-24)
  • Study Update
    • Dosing complete in MAD Cohort 1 through Cohort 3
    • Anticipate expansion into OTC heterozygotes to assess safety and CMP-CPS-001 effect on ureagenesis in patients with evidence of reduced urea cycle function

The presentations can be accessed on the CAMP4 website at https://investors.camp4tx.com/news-events/presentations after the presentations.

About CAMP4 Therapeutics

CAMP4 is developing disease-modifying treatments for a broad range of genetic diseases where amplifying healthy protein may offer therapeutic benefits. Our approach amplifies mRNA by harnessing a fundamental mechanism of how genes are controlled. To amplify mRNA, our therapeutic ASO drug candidates target regulatory RNAs (regRNAs), which act locally on transcription factors and are the master regulators of gene expression. CAMP4’s proprietary RAP Platform™ enables the mapping of regRNAs and generation of therapeutic candidates designed to target the regRNAs associated with genes underlying haploinsufficient and recessive partial loss-of-function disorders, of which there are more than 1,200, in which a modest increase in protein expression may have the potential to be clinically meaningful. For more information, visit camp4tx.com.

data.title: Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd Clarifies No Affiliation with Black Capital Partners
data.description:
LONDON, May 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd, a London-based boutique consultancy specialising in helping companies with investor introductions across private equity, venture capital, and real estate, today issued a public clarification regarding its independent status and operations.

The announcement follows ongoing market enquiries and online search activity that has led to confusion between Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd and another similarly named entity, Black Capital Partners. It has come to their attention that recent online searches such as "Black Castle Capital Reviews" have inadvertently associated Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd with unrelated content pertaining to Black Capital Partners, while these are entirely separate businesses with no shared ownership, leadership, or operational overlap.

The clarification aims to support market transparency, ensure accurate information for investors and partners, and reaffirm the distinct position of Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd in the consultancy space.

Statement from the CEO. 

 "We regret the confusion caused by this naming similarity and urge clients, partners and the public to verify information directly through our official channels. We welcome due diligence and our track record speaks for itself,” said Richard Diaz, CEO at Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd. 

Background: Distinguishing Two Independent Companies

Recent market enquiries have highlighted instances where Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd have been mistakenly associated with Black Capital Partners, a totally unrelated entity with separate leadership, branding, and business objectives. 

Why the Distinction Matters 

  • Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd operates independently from Black Capital Partners and any similarly named entities. 
  • Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd is registered with Companies House Number 10635644 under UK law. 
  • Clear distinction between entities helps avoid unnecessary confusion among investors and partners.
  • Avoiding confusion helps uphold brand integrity and informed decision-making.

 About Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd 

Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd is a boutique consultancy company based in London. The company was founded in 2017 by CEO and Founder, Richard Diaz, to provide bespoke solutions for entrepreneurs, growth-stage companies and institutional investors working alongside venture capital, private equity and strategic capital. 

The company partners with visionary founders and disruptive businesses to unlock long-term value. 

With a lean, hands-on approach, Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd distinguishes itself through sector-agnostic expertise, targeting high-potential opportunities in technology, real estate and emerging markets. The team offer not just help in securing capital through its introductory services but strategic guidance, network access and operational support.

Headquartered in London, Black Castle Capital Partners Ltd prides itself on flexibility, discretion and alignment of interests with its partners, high-net-worth individuals and institutional investors. The company's ethos revolves around building relationships ensuring each engagement is tailored to the unique ambitions of its partners.

data.title: Bitget Wallet Surpasses 80 Million Users Amid Rising Demand for Self-Custody
data.description:
SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador, May 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Bitget Wallet, the leading non-custodial crypto wallet, has surpassed 80 million users globally, marking a major milestone in its seventh year of operations. The sharp growth reflects a broader shift toward self-custody amid rising demand for mobile-first, multi-functional crypto wallets. As onchain activity continues to accelerate, wallets are emerging as central platforms in the evolving Web3 stack, driven by stablecoin adoption, real-world asset tokenization and regulatory softening.

Since its founding in 2018, Bitget Wallet has integrated with thousands of dApps and networks, processed over 250 million transactions, and facilitated over $12 billion in cumulative transaction volume. The wallet has evolved from a simple tool into a comprehensive hub for all onchain activities — from trading and earning to spending crypto in daily life. "Our vision has always been to make crypto practical and accessible to everyone," said Alvin Kan, COO of Bitget Wallet. "We're building a simple, seamless experience that helps users participate in Web3 on their own terms—from discovering new tokens to making real-world payments."

The adoption is being driven by a combination of mobile-first usage patterns, demand for asset sovereignty, and market volatility that is prompting users to seek greater control over their funds. Bitget Wallet's integrated approach—combining trading, asset management, and payments — has proven especially appealing to retail users navigating fragmented ecosystems. Its growth over the past year has also been fueled by a surge in onchain trading activity, alongside the launch of new payment features and global incentive campaigns. A series of product upgrades have helped users seize fast-moving opportunities, while simplified interfaces to attract new users across both emerging and developed markets.

Recent upgrades have further positioned Bitget Wallet as a central gateway for onchain engagement. The launch of Super DEX, a next-generation aggregator spanning over 130 chains, improves trade execution by tapping into deeper liquidity across networks. Bitget Wallet Alpha has emerged as a go-to mobile dashboard for real-time token signals and early-stage trading, helping users act quickly on emerging opportunities. On the payment front, the rollout of Shop with Crypto and PayFi integrations allows users to spend assets directly from their wallets, closing the gap between onchain value and everyday use. With growing activity, the wallet has also introduced enhanced security features, including default MEV protection and EIP-7702 detection tools, to ensure safer interactions at scale.

Now in its seventh year, Bitget Wallet is marking the milestone with a global campaign spotlighting its evolution into a more intuitive and widely used self-custody platform. The company will host community meetups in key regions, stream leadership sessions, and release a year-in-review to deepen engagement and expand its global presence.
